channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Los porteros no necesitan tu compasión
item.description: Aislado, expuesto y casi siempre juzgado por una sola jugada, el arquero encarna la parte más cruel y más emocionante del juego.
item.dc:creator: Tim Howard

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración fotográfica de The New York Times

item.title: La paradoja del trabajo que ayuda a la IA a acabar con trabajos
item.description: Un puñado de empresas emergentes ha encontrado una mina de oro desoladora: contratar a profesionales especializados para que ayuden a que los trabajos en sus campos se vuelvan obsoletos.
item.dc:creator: Lora Kelley

item.title: ¿Venezuela ahora es una colonia de EE. UU. o un Estado soberano?
item.description: La situación actual de Venezuela refleja la nueva era imperial, en la que es cada vez más innecesario e inconveniente para las grandes potencias hacer ocupaciones formales de Estados pequeños.
item.dc:creator: Linda Kinstler

item.title: Before Lettuce Felled Thousands, a Cilantro-Linked Cyclospora Outbreak Sickened New Yorkers
item.description: New York City health officials shared details of how they traced a much smaller cyclosporiasis outbreak, showing the difficulty of tracing contamination.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: The New York City health department investigated a smaller cyclosporiasis outbreak earlier this year.

item.title: Black and Latino Leaders Ask: What Do Socialist Wins Mean for New York?
item.description: Longtime leaders and advocates in minority districts fear that victories by the Democratic Socialists of America in gentrifying neighborhoods could mean political erasure.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A watch party for Representative Adriano Espaillat, a five-term incumbent and the first formerly undocumented person elected to Congress. He was among the politicians who lost to a democratic socialist in the primaries.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 19, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: El proyecto de ley para sancionar a Rusia plantea un dilema para Trump
item.description: El gobierno de Trump busca reducir las sanciones a medida que sus adversarios desarrollan alternativas que algunos creen podrían erosionar la supremacía del dólar de EE. UU.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Office of Foreign Assets Control (United States Treasury)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El Departamento del Tesoro en Washington

item.title: How We Watched the World Cup
item.description: The number of people who attend the World Cup is nothing compared with the sheer world-buckling biomass of those who experience it on a screen.
item.dc:creator: Mark O’Connell

item.title: It’s No Longer So Much Fun to Be MAGA in Washington
item.description: It’s a vibe shift.
item.dc:creator: Sally Quinn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: In Maine, Troy Jackson Gains Momentum in Bid to Replace Platner
item.description: Troy Jackson, a progressive former State Senate president, showed signs of significant strength, as half the state’s counties picked delegates to a convention where they will choose Graham Platner’s replacement.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner, Sydney Cromwell and Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: John Tully for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters met to select delegates who will represent their county to choose the nominee for U.S. Senate at the Maine Democratic Party’s Nominating Convention.

item.title: Why Tokyo’s New Shorts Policy is Sparking Debate in Japan
item.description: The Tokyo government is allowing workers to wear shorts for the first time this summer, prompting delicate questions about etiquette and hairy legs.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno

media:content.media:credit: Noriko Hayashi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Two officials wearing shorts at the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building, on Tuesday.

item.title: Tokyo Officials Ditch Suits for Shorts in Summer Heat
item.description: Tokyo’s government has a new summer dress code allowing shorts and T-shirts at work. But the initiative, meant to save energy and add comfort, has prompted a debate on office etiquette.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik and Noriko Hayashi

media:content.media:credit: Noriko Hayashi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Two officials wearing shorts at the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building, on Tuesday.

item.title: Corrections: July 19, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, July 19, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: In the Tournament’s Final, MetLife Stadium Faces a Monumental Last Test
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, June 19, 2026.

item.title: Photographer of That Messi-Baby Yamal Photo Tells the Backstory
item.description: Joan Monfort, a freelance photographer, took the now famous photo of the Argentine soccer legend Lionel Messi, then just 20, with Lamine Yamal, an infant who would grow up to become a phenom for Spain. Some 19 years later, the two are set to face off in the World Cup final on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

item.title: Vance Remarks on Epstein and Israel Echo Conspiracy Theories
item.description: The vice president’s comment that there were ties between Mr. Epstein and the “highest levels” of Israeli intelligence troubled some in his party.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Lisa Lerer

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance met with House Republicans on Capitol Hill this week.

item.title: Tate Brothers Arrested in Miami As U.K. Authorities File Sex Crime Charges
item.description: Federal marshals arrested the influencers, who are known for their misogynistic views and have faced years of criminal investigations in Europe. The new charges concern offenses dating to 2010, British officials said.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai, Megan Twohey and Mack Liederman

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Julia Demaree Nikhinson
media:content.media:description: Influencers Andrew Tate (right) and his brother Tristan Tate have developed a following for content that promotes hyper-masculinity. They have faced investigations over sexual abuse allegations.

item.title: What to Know About Fires Caused by Lightning in the Pacific Northwest
item.description: Dozens of fires ignited in Oregon and Washington as more than 14,000 lightning strikes hit the region during thunderstorms this week.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto and Amy Graff

item.title: Adidas Hopes Its World Cup Moment Can Drive U.S. Sales
item.description: Nearly everything on the field on Sunday will bear the German company’s logo. Bjørn Gulden, the chief executive, says he ‘couldn’t have scripted it better.’
item.dc:creator: Kim Bhasin

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bjørn Gulden, the Adidas chief executive. The World Cup has served as a springboard for the German company’s ambitions in the United States.

item.title: Two U.S. Service Members Killed In Iranian Strike, U.S. Military Says
item.description: Iran struck a military base in Jordan on Friday, killing two U.S. service members, according to the U.S. military. Sixteen military personnel have died in the war since the U.S. and Israel launched attacks on Iran in February.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: A satellite image showing Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Azraq, Jordan, in February. The base is one of a number in Jordan and the broader Gulf region that Iran said it had targeted with airstrikes this week.

item.title: Raising Capital
item.description: You’ll really have to step it up to solve Michael Lieberman’s latest Sunday puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: A llama in the mist at Machu Picchu, possibly thinking, How did I get here?

item.title: After Days of Wildfire Smoke, New York City Hit By Heavy Thunderstorms
item.description: Heavy rains and thunderstorms hit New York City on Saturday, prompting flash flood warnings for Manhattan, Brooklyn and Queens.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

item.title: Why Jordan Is Becoming a New Focus in the U.S.-Iran War
item.description: Four Iranian attacks in five days have targeted U.S. troops in Jordan, U.S. officials said, including one on Friday that killed two soldiers and left another service member missing.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Julian E. Barnes and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: American and Jordanian forces took part in multinational military exercises hosted by Jordan in 2022.

item.title: Madison Square Garden Sues Wired Magazine Over L.G.B.T.Q. Tracking Report
item.description: The Manhattan venue claims a July article from the technology magazine falsely implied that it tracked information about some celebrities’ sexual orientation for “discriminatory purposes.”
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald and Mack Liederman

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Wired reported in a July 9 article that the venue maintained a talent database containing information about the gender identity and sexual orientation of some V.I.P. guests.

item.title: Canada Wildfires: What to Know
item.description: Hundreds of wildfires in Canada and the United States have blanketed large sections of North America in dense smoke over the past week.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The observation deck of Toronto’s CN Tower on Wednesday as wildfire smoke enveloped downtown.

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Hangs Over World Cup Final
item.description: Smoke has blanketed New York City and other parts of the Northeast United States in a thick haze this week, unleashing smoggy skies and dangerous air.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman

media:content.media:description: Smoke from the Canadian wildfires surrounded MetLife Stadium in New Jersey on Saturday.

item.title: Lillian Roberts, Crusading New York Labor Leader, Dies at 98
item.description: She unionized state hospitals and battled with Mayor Michael R. Bloomberg during her feisty leadership of the nation’s largest municipal workers union.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams
item.category: American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees

media:content.media:description: Lillian Roberts in 2003 at her office at District Council 37. After a long absence, she had returned to lead the powerful union the previous year, when she was well into her 70s.

item.title: Cindy Burbank, Nebraska’s Democratic Senate Nominee, Files to Withdraw From Race
item.description: The move by Cindy Burbank could clear a path for Dan Osborn, an independent candidate backed by her party’s leadership. But Republicans have cried foul, and her effort could face obstacles.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Walker Pickering for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cindy Burbank in May.

item.title: Deaths of 2 U.S. Troops in Jordan Brings American Toll in the War With Iran to 16
item.description: President Trump vowed to avenge the deaths of any U.S. military personnel.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A satellite image showing Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Azraq, Jordan, in February. The base is one of a number in Jordan and the broader Gulf region that Iran said it had targeted with airstrikes this week.

item.title: Joe Melson, 91, Dies; Wrote ‘Only the Lonely’ and Other Hits With Roy Orbison
item.description: Working together closely, the pair turned out more than 200 songs, including “Crying,” “Running Scared” and “Blue Bayou.”
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Ukraine’s Top General Becomes a Target of Protesters’ Anger
item.description: Demonstrators supporting Ukraine’s ousted defense minister have begun denouncing the commander he clashed with in office, Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer and Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People in Kyiv, Ukraine, on Thursday protesting President Volodymyr Zelensky’s decision to oust Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who had championed drone warfare.

item.title: Lamine Yamal, astro de fútbol e hijo de migrantes, personifica una España cambiante
item.description: Mientras se prepara para la final del Mundial, Yamal, de 19 años, está en el centro de un debate nacional sobre lo que significa ser español en una sociedad cada vez más multicultural.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz

media:content.media:credit: Maria Contreras Coll para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una multitud cerca de Rocafonda, el barrio donde creció Lamine Yamal, observa al jugador en una pantalla gigante durante la semifinal de España contra Francia la semana pasada.

item.title: Wildfires Continue To Burn in Ontario, Canada, Sending Smoke to U.S. Cities
item.description: Nearly 200 fires continued to devastate swaths of the province as smoke continued to drift across parts of Canada and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Premier Doug Ford of Ontario after a news conference in Thunder Bay on Saturday. Roughly 1.7 million acres have burned in Ontario, data from the Canadian Interagency Forest Fire Center shows.

item.title: Thunderstorms, Flooding and Strong Winds Sweep the Northeast
item.description: Forecasters warned of winds over 70 miles per hour in New York City, Philadelphia, Baltimore and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A box truck with its passengers was caught in flash flooding on the BQE underpass at 65th Place on Saturday.

item.title: Mike Lindell Not Registered to Vote in Minnesota, Where He is Running for Governor
item.description: President Trump endorsed Mike Lindell, a leader of the election denial movement, for Minnesota governor this week.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: Mike Lindell during the Conservative Political Action Conference, in Grapevine, Texas, in March.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Leave Iranians Isolated and Scared
item.description: Overnight strikes damaged a tunnel and three bridges in Hormozgan Province, the governor’s office said.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The Persian Gulf off the coast of Bandar Abbas, an Iranian port city in the south of the country, in May. Residents said roads around the city were heavily damaged by U.S. airstrikes this week.

item.title: Your ‘Live’ World Cup Broadcast Is Not Really Live
item.description: Everyone is watching the goals after they happen, and some get them significantly faster.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather

media:content.media:description: Lautaro Martinez of Argentina scored the winning goal over England on Wednesday. When did you see it?

item.title: Ketanji Brown Jackson Knows What 1776 Meant
item.description: The revolutionary meaning of the Declaration of Independence did not elude her.
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie

item.title: پشت پرده عملیات محرمانه اسرائیل برای جذب همکاری احمدی نژاد
item.description: تلاش چندین ساله اسرائیل برای تبدیل رئیس جمهور پیشین ایران به یک مهره اطلاعاتی در روزهای نخست جنگ به عملیاتی پیچیده برای انتقال او به خانه امن سازمان موساد در ایران منجر شد. اما این نقشه به شکست انجامید.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: کپشن عکس: محمود احمدی نژاد، رئیس جمهور سابق ایران، به تسریع برنامه هسته‌ ای ایران و مواضع ضد اسرائیلی‌ اش شناخته می‌شد. عکاس: تاد هایسلر/نیویورک تایمز

item.title: A Very British CPAC: Few, if Any, Costumes and Not Much Trump
item.description: London hosted an understated version of the Conservative Political Action Conference, known in the United States for its over-the-top celebration of MAGA causes.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:description: Matt Schlapp, chairman of the Conservative Political Action Conference, and the former prime minister Liz Truss onstage at CPAC’s London conference on Thursday.

item.title: Garlic Bread Grilled Cheese, of Course
item.description: The precise sandwich to serve with a plate of juicy tomatoes or crisp cucumbers.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Ghazalle Badiozamani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Brett Regot.
media:content.media:description: Kayla Hoang’s garlic bread grilled cheese.

item.title: Taylor Farms Expands Iceberg Lettuce Recall to 27 States Amid Cyclospora Outbreak
item.description: The F.D.A. said that a new sample from the company’s Mexico facility had tested positive for cyclospora and that the agency was trying to trace affected products.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Julie Creswell
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: Taylor Farms — like other food producers — does not disclose which grocery stores or restaurants it distributes leafy greens or other vegetables to.

item.title: Uproar Over Surrogate Baby Prompts German Official’s Sudden Resignation
item.description: Jens Spahn, a top leader of Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s party, resigned his post after announcing that he had a baby by surrogate, which is illegal in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley

media:content.media:description: Jens Spahn, parliamentary group leader of Germany’s Christian Democratic Union party, in the plenary hall during a session at the Bundestag, Germany’s lower house of Parliament, in December.

item.title: Peak Mango Season Is Upon Us, Thank Goodness
item.description: I’m making mango shortcakes, mango lime loaf cake and mango soft serve.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Armando Rafael for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jocelyn Delk Adams’s mango lime loaf cake.

item.title: Sí, los terapeutas de pareja también pelean con su pareja. Así es como lo hacen
item.description: Estos expertos pasan sus días ayudando a las personas a resolver conflictos. Aquí algunas de sus estrategias.
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn

item.title: These AIPAC-Backed Democrats Voted to Nix Israel Aid. AIPAC Punished Them.
item.description: Of the 103 Democrats who voted to eliminate aid to Israel, more than a dozen had been endorsed by the pro-Israel lobbying group. AIPAC quickly closed off online donations for them.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:description: The U.S. Capitol this week.

item.title: Walt Odets, Who Delved Into the Minds of Gay Men, Dies at 79
item.description: A psychologist, he wrote during the AIDS crisis about survivor’s guilt and depression in his H.I.V.-negative patients.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel

item.title: The Hunt for a Job Has Never Been Worse. These Applicants Are Fighting Back.
item.description: A.I. is destroying the application process for employers and potential employees. We need to fix it.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Grose

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Elkin Jaramillo for The New York Times

item.title: The Evolution of Presidential Travel: Horse Carriages to Air Force One
item.description: From horse-drawn presidential coaches to Air Force One, presidents have used various modes of transportation to project an image of America for nearly 250 years.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Launches Strikes After Iranian Attack Kills 2 American Soldiers
item.description: The soldiers, killed in Jordan, were the first U.S. troops to die from hostile fire since an April truce. U.S. forces began retaliatory strikes early Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
